round_2_summary: Strong convergence. P0 shape-mismatch (deriveTodos uses {type:'tool-call', args} but live UIMessages are {type:'tool-<name>', input}, persisted is {type:'tool-call', args}, rehydrated via buildInitialMessages is {type:'dynamic-tool', input/output}) verified by architect, skeptic, test-strategy-auditor, aegis-fit-auditor with file:line. P0 telemetry pollution (agent.ts:87-108 onStepFinish writes every tool call to aegis_tool_executions; plan's 'NOT touching aegis_tool_executions' is false) verified by aegis-fit + architect + skeptic. P0 prompt-rule rewrite needed (prompt-design-auditor proposes structured When/Do-NOT/Lifecycle scaffold + negative examples; current copy contradicts itself on threshold; conflicts with existing fan-out rule). P0 convergent simplification (pragmatist proposes collapse to single set_todos full-replace tool; resolves id fabrication, naming asymmetry, step budget, prompt complexity, half the tests). P1s: stepCountIs(25) cap collision (12 todos = 25 calls exactly), MessageBubble empty-bubble guard missing, framer-motion verified NOT a dep, latest-assistant-message-vs-latest-message bug, tool-result-pairing-on-rehydration silent drop, message-history feedback loop replays UI bookkeeping into LLM context, set_todos suppression-ordering with request_fix error path. P2s: turn-boundary semantics, first-render scroll displacement, stop-button divergence (Loader2 spins after stop), discreteness threshold ambiguity, prose-suppression vs announce-mode conflict, FixPanel surface boundary, a11y not specified.

consensus_p0_findings.claim: deriveTodos algorithm matches none of the three real part shapes (live streaming, persisted, rehydrated). Strip will silently render nothing.
consensus_p0_findings.patch: Single shared normalizer in aegis-parts.ts mirroring MessageBubble's isToolPart + toolNameFor. Round-trip test against three fixture shapes.

consensus_p0_findings.claim: agent.ts onStepFinish writes every tool call to aegis_tool_executions unconditionally — 25 telemetry rows per multi-step turn for UI bookkeeping, contradicts plan's 'NOT touching' invariant.
consensus_p0_findings.patch: Optional audit?: false on AegisToolEntry, set_todos opts out. Plan must drop the 'NOT touching aegis_tool_executions' bullet and add agent.ts to Files Touched.

consensus_p0_findings.claim: Collapse to single set_todos({todos:[{title, status?}]}) full-list-replace tool resolves id fabrication, naming asymmetry, step budget, prompt complexity, ~6 of 8 derivation tests in one stroke.
consensus_p0_findings.patch: Single tool, full-replace semantics. Each call replaces the active list. No update_todo, no ids, no cancelled state, no reason field. Cursor + Claude Code both ship single-tool full-replace.
consensus_p0_findings.dissents: prompt-design-auditor + opportunity-scout: prefer batch update_todos (plural) for per-item progress prose without re-emitting full list. ux-walker: full-replace fine but mandates React keying by item.id.

consensus_p0_findings.claim: Sample prompt copy contradicts itself on threshold, conflicts with existing fan-out rule, contains UX-hostile silence rule, lacks negative examples. Prompt rule is the load-bearing artifact and is currently broken.
consensus_p0_findings.patch: Rewrite rule as structured When/Do-NOT/Lifecycle with concrete YES/NO + WRONG examples. Place as new rule 10 under new 'Plan' tool category. Drop 'don't return prose until done' clause. Threshold = ≥2 user-visible workstreams ≥30s each.

consensus_p0_findings.claim: single-tool-vs-two-tool is a load-bearing decision blocker — ~30% of R1 findings depend on it. Lock before /implement.
consensus_p0_findings.patch: Decide now. Recommended: single tool full-replace per pragmatist-r2-f1.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: stepCountIs(25) at agent.ts:77; 12 todos × 2 tools = 25 = exact cap, zero room for actual work.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Either single-tool collapse (drops calls to ~7 for 6 todos), batch update_todos, OR raise stopWhen to 40 + lower maxItems to 6/8.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: framer-motion VERIFIED NOT in package.json. Plan's hedge 'most likely a dependency' is wrong; motion.div would not compile.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Drop framer-motion. Use Tailwind transition-opacity + transition-colors. Lock in plan.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: messages[length-1] returns user reply most often; strip flickers out on every send.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Walk backward to most recent role==='assistant' message.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: Suppressing set_todos in MessageBubble produces empty assistant bubble when turn parts are only set_todos+update_todo (no text).
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Guard at end of MessageBubble: if !isUser && !error && elements.length===0 return null. Specify suppression order: BEFORE request_fix branch, AFTER toolName computed.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: Tool-call parts replay into LLM context on subsequent turns; UI bookkeeping bloats context. Compounds with stepCountIs and BYOK cost.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Filter set_todos parts when rebuilding ModelMessages for next turn (in stream route).

consensus_p1_findings.claim: tool-call parts without paired tool-result are silently dropped on rehydration (parts.ts:48); refresh-during-streaming = empty strip.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Either special-case set_todos in buildInitialMessages, or document rehydration limitation explicitly.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: Stalled state (stream ended, errors, user hits Stop, BYOK rate-limit) leaves Loader2 spinning forever — UI lies about ongoing work.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: When useChat.status !== 'streaming' AND any todo non-terminal, render rows muted (Pause icon, 'Stopped'/'Error' caption). Add header X dismiss affordance.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: Plan under-specifies test surface: missing prompt-snapshot test, MessageBubble regression, ChatTodos component test, page-refresh integration, turn-boundary, stalled-state. Solo manual dogfood is insufficient for CI.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Add prompt-snapshot assertion in aegis-v3-helpers.test.ts. Add MessageBubble.test.tsx. Add ChatTodos.test.tsx. Add backend round-trip test asserting persisted shape matches deriveTodos input. Reject scope-cutter's 'tests are bloat' framing for items where regression hole is real.
consensus_p1_findings.dissents: scope-cutter dissents heavily — argues most are 'testing the json-schema library' or 'test-debt the touching PR pays'.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: Turn-boundary undefined: when next turn happens with no own set_todos, are old todos retired? Plan ambiguous.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Sticky-across-turns ONLY between user-send and assistant-first-action. Once new assistant turn produces tool calls or text without own set_todos, retire prior-turn todos.

consensus_p1_findings.claim: Negative-example injection (Cursor/Claude Code pattern) missing — without WRONG examples with rationale, model has weaker discriminative signal.
consensus_p1_findings.patch: Append <wrong>...</wrong> blocks to rule 10 with concrete miscalls and 'WRONG because...' rationale.

consensus_p2_findings.claim: Plan has 4 unresolved open questions ('confirm with Henry on tasks reuse', terminal-state visibility TBD, operating-mode gate, framer-motion). All resolvable now.
FixPanel split-screen + chat strip both visible = duplicate-surface confusion. Reuse of FixListBody visual recipe makes them look identical.
False-start noise: agent emits set_todos for single-step requests, strip flickers in/out. Need minItems:2 in schema + minimum visible duration.
A11y not specified. Strip needs aria-live='polite' on counter only, role='status', aria-label.
Tools should explicitly set danger:'safe' to match convention (fix.ts:482-488).
Model-fabricated ids could leak to UI via tooltip/aria-label. Hard rule: ids never user-visible.
If single-tool collapse adopted, 'set_todos' name conflicts with fix-worker's durable task vocabulary. Consider set_chat_plan / set_turn_todos to disambiguate.

consensus_p3_findings_and_opportunities.claim: makeChatTool helper-factory pattern eliminates duplication for safe-tool entries.
While extracting derivation, also extract MessageBubble's isToolPart/toolNameFor/getInput/getOutput into shared aegis-parts.ts.
On terminal state, persist a compact 'Plan complete' summary chip into message body so chat history shows what happened.
OPEN DEBATE: drop Task 9 polish entirely (scope-cutter+pragmatist) OR keep 1.5s 'Done — N/N' hold (ux-walker+opportunity-scout). Disputed.
Use audit?: boolean (default true) on AegisToolEntry instead of separate CHAT_ONLY_TOOLS allowlist.

open_debates.recommendation: Collapse to single set_todos full-replace per pragmatist-r2-f1. Token cost concern is mitigated by maxItems:6/8. Lifecycle and note? concerns are addressable in v1.1 when actual usage data exists.

open_debates.topic: drop Task 9 polish vs keep Done hold
open_debates.recommendation: Keep just the Done — N/N hold (1.5s). Drop tooltip and truncation (already covered).

open_debates.topic: drop cancelled status vs keep for redirect
open_debates.recommendation: If single-tool collapse adopted, redirect = re-emit set_todos with shorter list. Drop cancelled. (Aligned with collapse decision.)
Cut json-schema-library tests and component-render-only tests. KEEP: prompt-snapshot test, MessageBubble regression for empty bubble, derivation round-trip across 3 shapes, page-refresh integration.

open_debates.topic: user recovery affordance (Dismiss X / per-row mark)
open_debates.recommendation: Header Dismiss-X (client-only, doesn't mutate parts) is acceptable v1. Per-row manual edit deferred to v1.1.
